reviews.review_description:
Hi Everyone - Hi to the devs,

I only rate this game 3 stars as there are still some technical issues triggering a lot of frustration in game for me - Mind, I'm going to bash open doors here as most of the complains I have have already been mentioned by other players.
As a former amateur boxer, I jumped straight into the gym for a bit of training to see whether I could get a bit of sweat going on and enjoy the game mechanics.

I find the tracking issues to be the most frustrating:
- when you get some padwork (with Balboa THAT is awesome), it's not clear why some punches do register and some don't. (speed? hands positioning?) Following advice I found online, I tried to keep my hands well inside the tracking area of the device, but still, I found my gloves weren't where I wanted them to be way too often. (or is it my muscle memory that makes me position my hands too close to my head, hence losing track, without even realising it?)
- when I move freely in the gym with the intention of picking and choosing a workout, I tend to position myself in relation to my real life reach. Starting a minigame just messes up your reach, distance and placement towards the target. I find that the default starting position is way too close forcing me to step back - and therefore messing your Guardian setup.
It might be my boxing style that doesn't work great with the game, but I find it difficult to keep the distance, as the game keeps pushing the target right in front of your face when you're just trying to get your reach right.
I'd much prefer it if the game would allow to trust the player to position themselves properly and not force their positioning system onto you.

So overall, it's a good game, hats off to the devs! But players that know a minimum about boxing, sparred and trained in real life, will find the limitations frustrating.

Let's hope the devs come up with a nice update and Oculus fixes that tracking issue soon!

Best, A.
Very hard
Can't we all just get along?

This demo is quite fun and a good workout.

I did not notice any tracking problems on a Quest 2 that other people report.

I do however, get slightly motion sick when the character moves forward or during the 'sprinting' recovery mechanic, so do be warned!
That's the only reason I am not giving this 5 stars.
Had to lie down for an hour.  Do not download this garbage.

No matter what I do in the settings I can no long walk in a straight line in the ring. The walking mechanics work great in the gym but as soon as I get into the ring I start walking backwards and to the side. Its incredibly frustrating and makes the game unplayable. I really loved this game when I got it but I cant recommend it anymore.

reviews.review_title: Good, but sound could be better.
reviews.review_description: This game demo is good on the Oculus Quest but it doesn't have surround sound. It seems like this should be corrected and could be corrected. Overall the game is fairly similar to the Oculus Rift version, but the freedom of the Oculus Quest headset makes it better than the Oculus Rift version (except for the sound).

reviews.review_title: Creed Full Vers Error Performing Query
reviews.review_description: This is my last resort!  I had to leave a negative review here on the demo because I cannot add a review to the full version.  Since the launch of the Quest store I’ve received an error performing query when trying to add Creed full version to my library. I have created ticket with Oculus support and they are unable to resolve the issue as well.  I have contacted the developer Survios via Twitter and Facebook with no response now.  Please someone fix this as I have received responses on Reddit that others are having this same issue!  I really want to play Creed and have it free through cross-buy but have no way to add it to my library because of the error.  It gives the same error on both iOS and Quest headset by the way.  This is not an issue on my Rift only the quest version.  Any help is greatly appreciated!!

I never played the full game because the demo was enough for me. I love the game for the mechanic is shows off for VR especially for new people. Super fun fighting that jacked dude, I will saw once you're in the fight you forget the mechanics lol block, dodge, punch.

This gave my arms a good workout and it was enjoyable to play as well. By the end of just the first round i was exhausted.  

I hope it was registering my hits with accurate strength but it's hard to tell.  

I did get motion sick when my character moved forward to meet my opponent before the fight.  But i have learned to just keep my eyes closed for a few seconds while that happens and that solved the problem.
it was nice but my circle didnt appear and i hit my arm with full force on drawer.. hopefully my controlers are okay

This is working way better than I thought! As soon as I get my paycheck I will buy the full game.
Awesomeness!

What's the point? Your opponent is in your face all the time, you land 50 hits but then it lands a knockout on the first hit. Fun... And when you've finished, you get retry or buy game. I want to EXIT!
really fun demo im considering on buying the full game sometime :)

reviews.review_title: I loved the game but i injured my wrist
reviews.review_description: I had badly set the room scale and ended up hitting a wooden wardrobe with pull power :/

Booted this up on my Q2 and after a little glitch that had me standing about a foot high, I rebooted and it worked like a charm. Amazing sense of realism and scale. The game has some of the best graphics I've seen yet. I did feel sometimes my "gloves" weren't quite doing what my hands were doing, and there's only just one short sample fight in the demo, but overall it was still pretty fun and gave me a bit of a workout.
Lots of bugs specially when walking or with the camera resets, it could have been a great game. It is not finished.
It's a good demo that shows you the game although it could be better.

There SHOULD be a warning about the health risk of this game. EVEN this DEMO!!!

I thought I was in fairly decent shape, and WOW, was I wrong. I was demonstrating the Headset to someone and picked this Demo to Cast.  Within minutes I was keeled over trying to knock out this HUGE GUY. In a DEMO???

I was bent over, breathing hard, and seriously, I've had irregular heartbeats for 5 days now, ever since I tried this. I'm quite worried now because all other vitals are normal.

PLEASE - Be sure you're in good enough shape for something this intense.  Hopefully they put up a warning and make the demo and first fights of this game QUITE a bit less taxing.  Otherwise, it could be disastrous.

Tons of potential, but the game just doesn’t work right. I obviously blocked and dodged punches yet somehow I am getting hit and knocked out. Hopefully the developers can fix this game, because if these issues can be fixed it could be great. I was looking forward to this game, but I am glad that I didn’t buy it.
